items.prompt: Decide whether the table `(2, 6), (4, 12), (7, 21)` is proportional and justify the constant of proportionality.
items.expected_evidence: justify congruence through rigid-motion reasoning and similarity through dilation or proportional correspondence
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates justify congruence through rigid-motion reasoning and similarity through dilation or proportional correspondence with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Find the unit rate for 18 miles in 3 hours and explain what it means.
items.expected_evidence: explain how scale factor changes side lengths while preserving angle structure
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates explain how scale factor changes side lengths while preserving angle structure with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Find 15% of 80 and explain how the percent is acting as a rate per hundred.
items.expected_evidence: identify corresponding features consistently across congruent and similar figures
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates identify corresponding features consistently across congruent and similar figures with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Explain why equal differences alone do not prove a relationship is proportional.
items.expected_evidence: justify congruence through rigid-motion reasoning and similarity through dilation or proportional correspondence
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to justify congruence through rigid-motion reasoning and similarity through dilation or proportional correspondence.

items.prompt: Explain how a unit rate and a constant of proportionality describe the same multiplicative structure.
items.expected_evidence: explain how scale factor changes side lengths while preserving angle structure
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to explain how scale factor changes side lengths while preserving angle structure.

items.prompt: A student says a table is proportional because it increases by 3 each row. Diagnose the additive-pattern error. Focus on correspondence ratio drift.
items.expected_evidence: justify congruence through rigid-motion reasoning and similarity through dilation or proportional correspondence
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward justify congruence through rigid-motion reasoning and similarity through dilation or proportional correspondence.

items.prompt: A student scales one quantity by 2 but the other by 3 and still claims proportionality. Identify the breakdown. Focus on scale factor meaning gap.
items.expected_evidence: explain how scale factor changes side lengths while preserving angle structure
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward explain how scale factor changes side lengths while preserving angle structure.

items.prompt: A map uses 1 cm for every 5 km. How far does 7.2 cm represent, and why is the relationship proportional?
items.expected_evidence: justify congruence through rigid-motion reasoning and similarity through dilation or proportional correspondence
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves justify congruence through rigid-motion reasoning and similarity through dilation or proportional correspondence in a changed surface form or representation.

items.prompt: A recipe uses 3 cups of flour for 2 batches. Write an equation relating flour to batches and find the amount for 5 batches.
items.expected_evidence: explain how scale factor changes side lengths while preserving angle structure
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves explain how scale factor changes side lengths while preserving angle structure in a changed surface form or representation.

items.prompt: On a delayed check, determine whether 4 notebooks for $6 and 10 notebooks for $15 are proportional and justify your reasoning.
items.expected_evidence: justify congruence through rigid-motion reasoning and similarity through dilation or proportional correspondence
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner still demonstrates justify congruence through rigid-motion reasoning and similarity through dilation or proportional correspondence on a delayed recheck without a blocking recurrence.

items.prompt: Probe transformations and coordinate symmetry before diagnosing congruence and similarity via transformations, and state whether the learner is missing multiplicative structure or only the current proportional application.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether transformations and coordinate symmetry is secure enough to support congruence and similarity via transformations
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in transformations and coordinate symmetry from a current-node issue in congruence and similarity via transformations.

items.prompt: Probe proportional relationships before diagnosing congruence and similarity via transformations, and state whether the learner is missing multiplicative structure or only the current proportional application.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether proportional relationships is secure enough to support congruence and similarity via transformations
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in proportional relationships from a current-node issue in congruence and similarity via transformations.
